# Consumer Goods - Full Documentation

# MuleSoft Accelerator for Consumer Goods

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

MuleSoft Accelerator for Consumer Goods helps consumer goods companies integrate their digital
operations to gain visibility into trade promotion management and retail execution. Our foundational
assets are expertly designed to jumpstart your top integration and business initiatives so that IT
teams can spend more time innovating and less time building from scratch.

## Use Cases

### Product and Account Integration with Hierarchies

The
[Product and Account Integration with Hierarchies](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-consumergoods/minor/5.3/pages/q2e-w7j/Use%20case%201%20-%20Product%20and%20Account%20Integration%20with%20Hierarchies/)
use case surfaces relevant product, customer, demand planning, and pricing information from ERP and
PIM systems and easily consolidate this information into Trade Promotion Management (TPM) platforms
like Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud for informed promotional planning.

### Retail Execution

The
[Retail Execution](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-consumergoods/minor/5.3/pages/Use%20case%202%20-%20Retail%20execution/)
use case unlocks shelf data to enable perfect retail execution with pre-built assets. Access store
audit data from image recognition software, like Trax, and smoothly integrate into Retail Execution
platforms, such as Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud, to monitor at shelf performance in one platform.

### Trade Promotion Effectiveness

The
[Trade Promotion Effectiveness](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-consumergoods/minor/5.3/pages/phi-ime/Use%20case%203%20-%20Trade%20promotion%20effectiveness/)
use case allows you to visualize your promotions in one integrated platform with pre-built assets
that integrate Business Intelligence Tools (BI Tools), such as CRM Analytics, into a TPM platform
like Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud.

### Trade Promotion Optimization

The
[Trade Promotion Optimization](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-consumergoods/minor/5.3/pages/uo5-830/Use%20case%204%20-%20Trade%20promotion%20optimization/)
use case allows you to visualize your promotions in one integrated platform with pre-built assets
that integrate Business Intelligence Tools (BI Tools), such as CRM Analytics, into a TPM platform
like Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud.

### POS Integration

The
[POS Integration](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-consumergoods/minor/5.3/pages/ebd-knq/Use%20case%206%20-%20POS%20integration/)
use case enables retail developers to leverage pre-built integrations to connect order and loyalty
data from Point of Sale (POS) systems to Data Cloud.

### Pricing Data integration for Trade Promotion Management

The
[Pricing Data Integration for TPM](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-consumergoods/minor/5.3/pages/3hl-pip/Use%20case%207%20-%20Pricing%20Data%20Integration%20for%20TPM/)
use case enables consumer packaged goods companies to more effectively analyze and optimize
promotional activities by integrating List Price and Cost of Goods Sold data with Hyperforce.

## See Also

- [Getting Started](../../accelerators-home/getting-started)
- [Prerequisites](prerequisites)
- [Release Notes](../../release-notes/accelerators/consumer-goods/consumer-goods-release-notes)
- [MuleSoft Accelerator for Consumer Goods on Exchange](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-consumergoods/)
---

# Prerequisites for MuleSoft Accelerator for Consumer Goods

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

This page provides a summary of the guides available for configuring the various systems and
products required to implement the use cases provided by this solution. The individual systems
needed, and amount of configuration required, depend on which assets are to be deployed in your
target environment(s) - refer to the use case pages for more details.

## Available Guides

The following setup and configuration guides are available for this accelerator:

- [SAP S/4HANA Setup Guide](sap-s4hana-setup-guide)

Most of the implementation templates also contain details on requirements specific to those
applications.

## See Also

- [Getting Started](../../accelerators-home/getting-started)
- [MuleSoft Accelerator for Consumer Goods](./)
---

# SAP S/4HANA Setup Guide

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The following describes how to configure and test an S/4HANA Cloud SOAP outbound service (Product
replication: `productmdmbulkreplicaterequest`) and the S/4HANA Cloud SOAP Connector as a Mule
application deployed on Cloudhub.

## Configurations in S/4HANA

To make the necessary configurations in S/4HANA:

1.  Create a **Communication System** in the corresponding Fiori app. This communication system
    represents the external system that will consume the S/4HANA Cloud SOAP service. In this
    example, itâs named "COM_0009_SOAP_OUT":
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-01.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-01.png)
2.  For the 'Business System' name, use the same name of the System ID.
3.  In the 'Host Name' field, enter the hostname of the Mule application deployed on Cloudhub (if
    you donât have it yet, you can enter a mock URL and then modify it later):
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-02.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-02.png)
4.  Create a user that will be used to connect to the Cloud Hub app for sending product information
    in the outbound scenario (this is necessary even though the Mule application does not validate
    the user; we used any user for outbound):
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-03.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-03.png)
5.  Use the **Communication Arrangement** Fiori app to create a new Communication Arrangement:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-04.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-04.png)
6.  Create a new **Communication Arrangement**, select the communication scenario "SAP_COM_0009",
    and then define a specific name for the arrangement, "SAP_COM_0009_SOAP".
7.  In the **Communication System** field, select the Communication System created in the previous
    step:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-05.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-05.png)
8.  For outbound communication, select the previously-created user and configure the **Product
    Master - Replicate from SAP S/4HANA Cloud to Client** and include the following settings. Make
    sure to note the Path (`/sap/bc/srt/scs/sap/productmdmbulkreplicaterequest`) because we will use
    this path to configure the Mule application SOAP endpoint:
    - Set the Service status to **Active**
    - Replication Model (any name)
    - Replication model = I (Initial)
    - The Output Mode = D (Direct).
      ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-06.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-06.png)
      ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-07.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-07.png)
      ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-08.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-08.png)

9.  Save it. All configurations at S/4HANA Cloud side are finished and you will be able to download
    the WSDL that contains the metadata to import into the S/4HANA Cloud SOAP Connector into the
    Mule application.

## Configurations in the Mule Application

The following example describes how to create a Mule application using the Outbound Message Listener
operation of MuleSoftâs SAP S/4HANA SOAP Connector:

1.  Create a Mule project and add the appropriate connector from Exchange to the Mule Palette:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-09.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-09.png)
2.  Add and configure the **Outbound Message Listener** to the canvas:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-10.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-10.png)
3.  Add the corresponding WSDL path to the WSDL you downloaded from S/4HANA Cloud and create the
    HTTP listener configuration:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-11.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-11.png)
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-12.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-12.png)
    _\*Note\*: we are using no authentication on the Mule Application, so we used the Insecure
    option for the Trust Store Configuration. In the Keystore configuration, we used a test
    Keystore, which is mandatory for HTTPS communication:_
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-13.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-13.png)
4.  Select the Message type that the connector expects to receive from SAP (the connector reads the
    metadata from the WSDL we configured):
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-14.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-14.png)
5.  On the Responses tab, use the status Code "202" and the Reason-Phrase "Accepted" because we want
    to send the same status that S/4HANA Cloud will send if the service invocation results OK. No
    payload will be returned as a responseââjust the Status code and Reason-phrase:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-15.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-15.png)
6.  Use a **Logger** component to log the inbound XML payload:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-16.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-16.png)
    _The Mule flow should look like the following:_
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-17.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-17.png)
7.  Deploy the application to Cloudhub. Youâre now ready to test the scenario.

## Testing the scenario

To test the scenario:

1.  Create the following. You only need to create a material in the corresponding Fiori app of
    S/4HANA Cloud:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-18.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-18.png)
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-19.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-19.png)
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-20.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-20.png)
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-21.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-21.png)
    _After you save the new material, you should see the following confirmation in S/4HANA Cloud:_
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-22.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-22.png)
    _The following shows the XML message received at the Mule application at the Runtime Manager on
    Cloudhub:_
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-23.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-23.png)
2.  See the sent message in the Message Dashboard Fiori Application:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-24.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-24.png)
3.  Select the date:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-25.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-25.png)
4.  Search under **CMD:Product Integration - /CMDPR â Product Replication to Client via Service -
    PRDREQ_OUT/00001 namespace**:
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-26.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-26.png)
    ![retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-27.png](https://www.mulesoft.com/ext/solutions/images/retail-sap-s4hana-setup-guide-27.png)

## S/4HANA customization guide

The following provides guidance on the custom fields that you must create for both the customer and
order sync use cases to function in an end-to-end manner.

Create the following new fields in the corresponding objects for the use case:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Salesforce Object</th><th>Field Name</th><th>Data Type</th><th>Length</th><th>Unique</th><th>ExternalId</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>BusinessPartner</p></td><td><p>YY1_SME_MPARTYROLEID_bus</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>36</p></td><td><p>Y</p></td><td><p>Y</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>SalesOrder</p></td><td><p>YY1_SME_MORDERID_SDH</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>36</p></td><td><p>Y</p></td><td><p>Y</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Product</p></td><td><p>YY1_SME_UnvProductId_PRD</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>36</p></td><td><p>Y</p></td><td><p>Y</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

The following provides guidance on the custom fields that you must create for both the customer and
product sync from SAP S/4HANA to Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud.

Create the following fields using the app Custom fields:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>SAP Field Name</th><th>Label</th><th>Data Type</th><th>Length</th><th>SAP Business Context</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_ParentID_RTL</p></td><td><p>Parent ID</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>10</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Business Partner</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_AccouTemp_RTL</p></td><td><p>Account template</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>50</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Business Partner</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_KAM_Prod_RTL</p></td><td><p>KAM Products</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>40</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_Comp_Prod_RTL</p></td><td><p>Competitive Product</p></td><td><p>Checkbox</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_Prod_Temp_RTL</p></td><td><p>Product template</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>50</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_Prod_Code_RTL</p></td><td><p>Product code</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>50 Master Data: Product General</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_Shrt_PCode_RTL</p></td><td><p>Short Product Code</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>40</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_Sell_Code_RTL</p></td><td><p>Sell in Code</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>40</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_Asset_Temp_RTL</p></td><td><p>Asset Template</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>20</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_PackSize_RTL</p></td><td><p>Pack Size</p></td><td><p>Decimal</p></td><td><p>12</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_PackSizeUn_RTL</p></td><td><p>Pack Size Unit</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>10</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_ProdForm_RTL Product Form</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>10</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_Category_RTL</p></td><td><p>Category</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>40</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_SubCategor_RTL</p></td><td><p>SubCategory</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>40</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_Brand_RTL</p></td><td><p>Brand</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>40</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_Flavor_RTL</p></td><td><p>Flavor</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>40</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_Package_RTL</p></td><td><p>Package</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>40</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_Prod_Desc1_RTL</p></td><td><p>Description 1</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>50</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_Prod_Desc2_RTL</p></td><td><p>Description 2</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>40</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_DlvValFrom_RTL</p></td><td><p>Delivery Valid From</p></td><td><p>Date</p></td><td><p>N/A</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_FieldValFr_RTL</p></td><td><p>Field Valid From</p></td><td><p>Date</p></td><td><p>N/A</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_KAMValFrom_RTL</p></td><td><p>KAM Valid From</p></td><td><p>Date</p></td><td><p>N/A</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_NewItmVldF_RTL</p></td><td><p>New Item Valid From</p></td><td><p>Date</p></td><td><p>N/A</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_FundValiFr_RTL</p></td><td><p>Fund Valid From</p></td><td><p>Date</p></td><td><p>N/A</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_DlvValThru_RTL</p></td><td><p>Delivery Valid Thru</p></td><td><p>Date</p></td><td><p>N/A</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_FieldValTh_RTL</p></td><td><p>Field Valid Thru</p></td><td><p>Date</p></td><td><p>N/A</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_KAMValThru_RTL</p></td><td><p>KAM Valid Thru</p></td><td><p>Date</p></td><td><p>N/A</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_NewItmVldT_RTL</p></td><td><p>New Item Valid Thru</p></td><td><p>Date</p></td><td><p>N/A</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_FundValiTh_RTL</p></td><td><p>Fund Valid Thru</p></td><td><p>Date</p></td><td><p>N/A</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_ManItemNmb_RTL</p></td><td><p>Manufacturer Item Number</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>20</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_EstimValue_RTL</p></td><td><p>Estimated Value</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>20</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_PowSuppCon_RTL</p></td><td><p>Power Supply Connected</p></td><td><p>Checkbox</p></td><td><p>N/A</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>YY1_SME_HierarchID_RTL</p></td><td><p>Hierarchy Identifier</p></td><td><p>Code List</p></td><td><p>2</p></td><td><p>Master Data: Product General</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

As YY1_SME_HierarchID_RTL data type is Code list, the possible values for are:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Code Value</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>CA</p></td><td><p>Category</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>SU</p></td><td><p>Subcategory</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>BR</p></td><td><p>Brand</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>FL</p></td><td><p>Flavor</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>PR</p></td><td><p>Product</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

On the section âUIs and Reportsâ for each field enable the corresponding UI app (Business Partner or
Product basic data) and the option OData extension model and publish.

To add the fields into each app, use the option Adapt UI from your profile.

## See Also

- [Prerequisites](prerequisites)
- [MuleSoft Accelerator for Consumer Goods](./)
